data.title: Urban Meyer joins Higginbotham to advise on expanding athletics practice with NIL insurance for college sports to protect athlete compensation commitments.
data.description: A firm based in Fort Worth, Texas called Higginbotham recently announced on January 14, 2026, the launch of new coverage options geared towards college programs and donor groups. These options are designed to safeguard the compensation commitments made to modern athletes. Higginbotham, known as a diversified insurance brokerage and financial services company, is rolling out a program known as NIL (Name, Image, and Likeness) to cater to this need. This initiative aims to provide protection for the financial welfare of current athletes in college programs.

data.title: Steven Bainbridge of Greenberg Traurig elected as judge of FIA Motorsports Tribunal & Court of Appeal.
data.description: In Dubai, UAE, on January 14, 2026, Steven Bainbridge, the co-chair of the Sports Business Industry Group at Greenberg Traurig, Limited, was appointed as a judge for the Fédération Internationale de l'Automobile (FIA) International Tribunal and International Court. This significant role will see him preside over crucial decisions within the realm of international auto sports governance.

data.title: Travel advisor named fastest-growing career, Nexion sees 20% interest surge. LinkedIn predicts growth in 2026.
data.description: According to LinkedIn's Jobs on the Rise 2026 report, the demand for travel advisors is increasing, indicating a resurgence in this field as a flexible and entrepreneurial career choice. The report highlights that travel advisor is among the fastest-growing careers in the United States. The surge in demand is evident from the increased advisory requests in various regions. This trend suggests promising opportunities for individuals interested in pursuing a career in travel advising.
France says will take part in European military mission to Greenland

data.title: Binance's spot trading dominance drops to 25% in Dec, down from peak of 60% in 2023. Offshore rivals gaining volume, with derivatives market share also declining. Spot trading accounts for 25% of all crypto transactions. Binance's derivatives market share peaked near 70% before dropping.
data.description: In December, Binance's spot trading dominance plummeted to 25%, its lowest level since January 2021. Offshore competitors Bybit, HTX, and Gate.io are gaining volume while on-chain platform Hyperliquid (HYPE) is revolutionizing trader leverage access. Binance's spot share has decreased by 58% from its 2023 peak, handling around 60% of spot crypto trades at its high before falling to 25% in December, down from 28.5% in November. This signifies the weakest position in four years for the exchange and indicates a shift in crypto's $3.2 trillion market trading locations. Normally, spot trading makes up about a quarter of crypto transactions, with the majority occurring in derivatives like perpetual futures. Binance also experienced a drop in its derivatives market share, which peaked near 70%. The exchange's current market share in derivatives stands at...

data.title: Hagens Berman investigates Alvotech (ALVO) for potential securities fraud after FDA-induced stock crash.
data.description: A national shareholder rights firm, Hagens Berman, is currently investigating Alvotech (NASDAQ: ALVO) about the accuracy of their disclosures related to the company's manufacturing operations and the regulatory standing of its main biosimilar contender. The inquiry comes amid concerns regarding potential misconduct surrounding these aspects of Alvotech's operations. Investors are encouraged to stay informed on the developments regarding this investigation into Alvotech, a company listed on the NASDAQ with the ticker symbol ALVO.

data.title: Cessna Citation M2 Gen2 with Garmin Autothrottles enters service, enhancing pilots' control. FAA certified in Oct 2025.
data.description: The Cessna Citation M2 Gen2, equipped with Garmin Autothrottles, has been launched in Wichita, Kansas. Approved by the FAA in October 2025, this upgrade enhances control and precision in the most popular light-entry jet. Manufactured by Textron Aviation Inc., a subsidiary of Textron Inc. (NYSE: TXT), this new system offers advanced capabilities to pilots.

data.title: City of North Miami Beach to honor Jeanette M. Nuñez with Key to the City for her contributions as President of FIU and former Lieutenant Governor.
data.description: In North Miami Beach, Florida on January 14, 2026, the Mayor and City Commission will bestow the Key to the City upon Honorable Jeanette M. Nuñez. She currently serves as the President of Florida International University and had previously held the position of Lieutenant Governor of Florida. It is a significant honor recognizing her contributions.

data.title: Ronald R. Gendreau appointed Chair of 5Star Life Insurance Company's board of directors, effective January 1, 2026.
data.description: 5Star Life Insurance Company (5Star Life) has appointed Ronald R. Gendreau as Chair of its board of directors, starting January 1, 2026. General Larry O. Spencer, USAF (Ret.), Chairman and President, expressed satisfaction with the decision, citing Gendreau's extensive industry expertise and familiarity with 5Star Life as valuable assets for the company's strategic direction.

data.title: Green Circle Decarbonize Tech Ltd. closed $10M IPO offering 2.5M shares at $4/share for total gross proceeds of $10M.
data.description: On January 14, 2026, Green Circle Decarbonize Technology Limited completed its initial public offering on the NYSE under the symbol GCDT. The offering involved 2,500,000 ordinary shares priced at $4.00 per share, resulting in gross proceeds of about $10,000,000. Underwriters were also given a 45-day option to acquire up to 375,000 more shares at the same price. Green Circle specializes in developing and producing Phase Change Material storage systems utilized in cooling and heating applications, primarily through its Hong Kong subsidiary, Boca International Limited.

data.title: Global quantum computing market expected to grow from $422M to $1.63B by 2030 with a CAGR of 14.5% - ResearchAndMarkets.com.
data.description: A recent report on the quantum computing market indicates significant growth potential, with the market expected to reach around USD 1.63 billion by 2030 from the current USD 422 million, showcasing a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 14.5% during the forecast period. The report examines the industry trends, global forecasts, and distribution based on the type of drug discovery service offered, therapeutic areas, and key geographical regions. This growth is driven by various factors, pointing towards a promising future for the quantum computing sector.

data.title: GOP pushes bill allowing lawmakers to keep stock; can sell with 7-14 days notice. Dems say it aids insider traders.
data.description: Under the proposed bill, legislators could retain their current stock holdings and make sales after giving a notice period of seven to 14 days. Critics from the Democratic party have labeled this provision as a favor to insider traders.

data.title: CUPE 4900 members reject tentative deal due to financial pressures; planning rally in Newmarket; offer didn't address affordability crisis.
data.description: In Newmarket, Ontario, Paramedics, roads workers, public health workers, and other members of CUPE 4900 are set to hold an information picket and rally outside the upcoming Committee of the Whole meeting. The picket serves as a strong indication of members' mobilization in response to the regional offer, which did not adequately address the significant financial challenges frontline workers are currently facing. Following the rejection of the tentative deal by members, it is evident that it did not effectively tackle the escalating affordability crisis that was highlighted in a recent CUP report.
